prompt: Is there a correlation between difficulty rating and average grade per course?

inputData.question: Is there a correlation between difficulty rating and average grade per course?

response.bodyRaw:
{"status":"failed","steps":{"input":{"question":"Is there a correlation between difficulty rating and average grade per course?"},"sql":{"payload":{"question":"Is there a correlation between difficulty rating and average grade per course?"},"startedAt":1771804497660,"status":"success","output":{"reasoning":"Compute per-course average difficulty from course_evaluations and per-course average numeric grade from numeric components in grades (exam/project/attendance). Then compute Pearson correlation and linear regression slope/intercept across courses that have both metrics. I treat per-enrollment grade as the average of the available numeric score components (ignore textual/letter grade_value).","sql":"WITH avg_eval AS (
  SELECT
    cs.course_id,
    AVG(ce.difficulty_rating)::numeric AS avg_difficulty,
    COUNT(*) AS eval_count
  FROM public.course_evaluations ce
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  GROUP BY cs.course_id
),
avg_grade AS (
  SELECT
    cs.course_id,
    AVG(
      (COALESCE(g.exam_score,0) + COALESCE(g.project_score,0) + COALESCE(g.attendance_score,0))
      / NULLIF(
          (CASE WHEN g.exam_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
          + (CASE WHEN g.project_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
          + (CASE WHEN g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),
          0
        )
    )::numeric AS avg_grade,
    COUNT(g.id) AS grade_count
  FROM public.grades g
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  GROUP BY cs.course_id
),
per_course AS (
  SELECT
    c.id AS course_id,
    c.code AS course_code,
    ae.avg_difficulty AS avg_difficulty,
    ag.avg_grade AS avg_grade,
    COALESCE(ae.eval_count,0) AS eval_count,
    COALESCE(ag.grade_count,0) AS grade_count
  FROM public.courses c
  LEFT JOIN avg_eval ae ON ae.course_id = c.id
  LEFT JOIN avg_grade ag ON ag.course_id = c.id
)
SELECT
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE avg_difficulty IS NOT NULL AND avg_grade IS NOT NULL) AS courses_with_both,
  corr(avg_difficulty, avg_grade) AS pearson_correlation,
  regr_slope(avg_grade, avg_difficulty) AS regr_slope_grade_on_difficulty,
  regr_intercept(avg_grade, avg_difficulty) AS regr_intercept_grade_on_difficulty
FROM per_course
WHERE avg_difficulty IS NOT NULL AND avg_grade IS NOT NULL;","assumptions":["Per-enrollment numeric grade is computed as the average of the available numeric components (exam_score, project_score, attendance_score); enrollments with none of these numeric components are excluded from grade averages.","Average difficulty is computed from course_evaluations joined through enrollments -> course_sections -> course; all evaluation rows are included (no deduplication by enrollment).","Correlation reported is the Pearson correlation across courses that have both an avg_difficulty and avg_grade.","grades.grade_value (user-defined, likely non-numeric) is ignored because it is not numeric.","Using public schema tables discovered at preflight; joins and column names were confirmed via schema inspection."]},"endedAt":1771804551941},"run-raw-sql":{"payload":{"sql":"WITH avg_eval AS (

input.question: Is there a correlation between difficulty rating and average grade per course?

payload.question: Is there a correlation between difficulty rating and average grade per course?

output.reasoning: Compute per-course average difficulty from course_evaluations and per-course average numeric grade from numeric components in grades (exam/project/attendance). Then compute Pearson correlation and linear regression slope/intercept across courses that have both metrics. I treat per-enrollment grade as the average of the available numeric score components (ignore textual/letter grade_value).
output.sql:
WITH avg_eval AS (
  SELECT
    cs.course_id,
    AVG(ce.difficulty_rating)::numeric AS avg_difficulty,
    COUNT(*) AS eval_count
  FROM public.course_evaluations ce
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  GROUP BY cs.course_id
),
avg_grade AS (
  SELECT
    cs.course_id,
    AVG(
      (COALESCE(g.exam_score,0) + COALESCE(g.project_score,0) + COALESCE(g.attendance_score,0))
      / NULLIF(
          (CASE WHEN g.exam_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
          + (CASE WHEN g.project_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
          + (CASE WHEN g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),
          0
        )
    )::numeric AS avg_grade,
    COUNT(g.id) AS grade_count
  FROM public.grades g
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  GROUP BY cs.course_id
),
per_course AS (
  SELECT
    c.id AS course_id,
    c.code AS course_code,
    ae.avg_difficulty AS avg_difficulty,
    ag.avg_grade AS avg_grade,
    COALESCE(ae.eval_count,0) AS eval_count,
    COALESCE(ag.grade_count,0) AS grade_count
  FROM public.courses c
  LEFT JOIN avg_eval ae ON ae.course_id = c.id
  LEFT JOIN avg_grade ag ON ag.course_id = c.id
)
SELECT
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE avg_difficulty IS NOT NULL AND avg_grade IS NOT NULL) AS courses_with_both,
  corr(avg_difficulty, avg_grade) AS pearson_correlation,
  regr_slope(avg_grade, avg_difficulty) AS regr_slope_grade_on_difficulty,
  regr_intercept(avg_grade, avg_difficulty) AS regr_intercept_grade_on_difficulty
FROM per_course
WHERE avg_difficulty IS NOT NULL AND avg_grade IS NOT NULL;
assumptions: Per-enrollment numeric grade is computed as the average of the available numeric components (exam_score, project_score, attendance_score); enrollments with none of these numeric components are excluded from grade averages.
Average difficulty is computed from course_evaluations joined through enrollments -> course_sections -> course; all evaluation rows are included (no deduplication by enrollment).
Correlation reported is the Pearson correlation across courses that have both an avg_difficulty and avg_grade.
grades.grade_value (user-defined, likely non-numeric) is ignored because it is not numeric.
Using public schema tables discovered at preflight; joins and column names were confirmed via schema inspection.

payload.sql:
WITH avg_eval AS (
  SELECT
    cs.course_id,
    AVG(ce.difficulty_rating)::numeric AS avg_difficulty,
    COUNT(*) AS eval_count
  FROM public.course_evaluations ce
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  GROUP BY cs.course_id
),
avg_grade AS (
  SELECT
    cs.course_id,
    AVG(
      (COALESCE(g.exam_score,0) + COALESCE(g.project_score,0) + COALESCE(g.attendance_score,0))
      / NULLIF(
          (CASE WHEN g.exam_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
          + (CASE WHEN g.project_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
          + (CASE WHEN g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),
          0
        )
    )::numeric AS avg_grade,
    COUNT(g.id) AS grade_count
  FROM public.grades g
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  GROUP BY cs.course_id
),
per_course AS (
  SELECT
    c.id AS course_id,
    c.code AS course_code,
    ae.avg_difficulty AS avg_difficulty,
    ag.avg_grade AS avg_grade,
    COALESCE(ae.eval_count,0) AS eval_count,
    COALESCE(ag.grade_count,0) AS grade_count
  FROM public.courses c
  LEFT JOIN avg_eval ae ON ae.course_id = c.id
  LEFT JOIN avg_grade ag ON ag.course_id = c.id
)
SELECT
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE avg_difficulty IS NOT NULL AND avg_grade IS NOT NULL) AS courses_with_both,
  corr(avg_difficulty, avg_grade) AS pearson_correlation,
  regr_slope(avg_grade, avg_difficulty) AS regr_slope_grade_on_difficulty,
  regr_intercept(avg_grade, avg_difficulty) AS regr_intercept_grade_on_difficulty
FROM per_course
WHERE avg_difficulty IS NOT NULL AND avg_grade IS NOT NULL;

  SELECT

    AVG(ce.difficulty_rating)::numeric AS avg_difficulty,
    COUNT(*) AS eval_count
  FROM public.course_evaluations ce
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  GROUP BY cs.course_id
),
avg_grade AS (
  SELECT
    cs.course_id,
    AVG(
      (COALESCE(g.exam_score,0) + COALESCE(g.project_score,0) + COALESCE(g.attendance_score,0))
      / NULLIF(
          (CASE WHEN g.exam_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
          + (CASE WHEN g.project_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
          + (CASE WHEN g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),
          0
        )
    )::numeric AS avg_grade,
    COUNT(g.id) AS grade_count
  FROM public.grades g
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  GROUP BY cs.course_id
),
per_course AS (
  SELECT
    c.id AS course_id,
    c.code AS course_code,
    ae.avg_difficulty AS avg_difficulty,
    ag.avg_grade AS avg_grade,
    COALESCE(ae.eval_count,0) AS eval_count,
    COALESCE(ag.grade_count,0) AS grade_count
  FROM public.courses c
  LEFT JOIN avg_eval ae ON ae.course_id = c.id
  LEFT JOIN avg_grade ag ON ag.course_id = c.id
)
SELECT
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE avg_difficulty IS NOT NULL AND avg_grade IS NOT NULL) AS courses_with_both,
  corr(avg_difficulty, avg_grade) AS pearson_correlation,
  regr_slope(avg_grade, avg_difficulty) AS regr_slope_grade_on_difficulty,
  regr_intercept(avg_grade, avg_difficulty) AS regr_intercept_grade_on_difficulty
FROM per_course
WHERE avg_difficulty IS NOT NULL AND avg_grade IS NOT NULL
